headline: Anthropic's Mythos release dominates the cycle while recommended channels press on cutting inference cost and tightening AI workflows.
body: Anthropic shipped the Mythos family, with Fable 5 (Mythos 5 plus guardrails) generally available and free on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22 before usage moves to credits [10], and Chase AI flags the benchmark numbers as significant [7]; Anthropic's own demos show Fable 5 playing Factorio autonomously [11], simulating the solar system to predict an eclipse [12], and syncing a coded fluid simulation to a Beethoven EDM remix [13]. The recommended inner circle leans toward efficiency and tooling: Better Stack covers Headroom, Netflix's open-source compressor that cuts agent token usage 60-95 percent via content-aware compressors and a local Kompress model [3], and Open Notebook, a self-hosted NotebookLM alternative with an API for indexing and grounded QA [0]. Caleb Writes Code breaks down MiniMax M3, which swaps full attention for sparse attention (MSA) plus tiled I/O and optimized KV cache reads to speed prefill and decode [4]. Better Stack also reports an Instagram AI flaw enabling account takeovers [2], argues single-file HTML is becoming the new Markdown [5], and demos free Claude Code access via Chipotle [1].
